ddls:
CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "published_datetime" TEXT /* Date and time when the event was published */,
  "event_start_datetime" TEXT /* Start date and time of the event */,
  "event_end_datetime" TEXT /* End date and time of the event */,
  "unavailability_type" INTEGER /* Type of unavailability (e.g., Planned, Unplanned) */,
  "available_capacity" INTEGER /* Available capacity during the event in MW */,
  "affected_unit" INTEGER /* Identifier of the affected unit */,
  "reason" INTEGER /* Reason for the unavailability */,
  "event_status" INTEGER /* Current status of the event (e.g., Active, Completed) */,
  "additional_details" TEXT /* Additional details or notes about the event */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* Who created the event record */,
  "last_updated_by" TEXT /* Who last updated the event record */,
  "last_updated_datetime" TEXT /* When the event record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_reason FOREIGN KEY ("reason") REFERENCES reasons ("reason_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_unavailability_type FOREIGN KEY ("unavailability_type") REFERENCES unavailability_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_affected_unit FOREIGN KEY ("affected_unit") REFERENCES units ("unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_event_status FOREIGN KEY ("event_status") REFERENCES event_statuses ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the power generation unit */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the unit */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the unit (e.g., Coal, Gas, Renewable) */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Total capacity of the unit in MW */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the unit was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_due" TEXT /* Date of the next maintenance due */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the unit (e.g., Operational, Under Maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "unavailability_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the unavailability type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the unavailability type */,
  "criticality" TEXT /* Criticality of the unavailability type (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the event status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event status */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the status is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reasons" (
  "reason_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reason */,
  "reason_description" TEXT /* Description of the reason for unavailability */,
  "criticality" TEXT /* Criticality of the reason (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reason_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "capacity_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each capacity log */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event for which the capacity is logged */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Available capacity during the event in MW */,
  "log_datetime" TEXT /* Date and time when the capacity was logged */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the capacity log (e.g., Manual, Automated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_capacity_logs_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event that triggered the notification */,
  "recipient" TEXT /* Recipient of the notification */,
  "notification_datetime" TEXT /* Date and time when the notification was sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "sent_by" TEXT /* Who sent the notification */,
  "sent_status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., Sent, Failed) */,
  "retry_count" INTEGER /* Number of retry attempts for failed notifications */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance log */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the unit for which the maintenance is logged */,
  "maintenance_start_datetime" TEXT /* Start date and time of the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_end_datetime" TEXT /* End date and time of the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., Routine, Emergency) */,
  "maintenance_description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance performed */,
  "technician" TEXT /* Name of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* Who created the maintenance log */,
  "created_datetime" TEXT /* When the maintenance log was created */,
  "last_updated_by" TEXT /* Who last updated the maintenance log */,
  "last_updated_datetime" TEXT /* When the maintenance log was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_logs_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_schedules" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance schedule */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the unit for which the maintenance is scheduled */,
  "scheduled_start_datetime" TEXT /* Scheduled start date and time of the maintenance */,
  "scheduled_end_datetime" TEXT /* Scheduled end date and time of the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., Routine, Emergency) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the scheduled maintenance */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* Who created the maintenance schedule */,
  "created_datetime" TEXT /* When the maintenance schedule was created */,
  "last_updated_by" TEXT /* Who last updated the maintenance schedule */,
  "last_updated_datetime" TEXT /* When the maintenance schedule was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_schedules_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES units ("unit_id")
)
